title: Ipomoea woronovii

The classification of Ipomoea woronovii is mainly based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis.

intro: Ipomoea woronovii, also known as Devil's Shoestring, is a perennial climber native to North America, belonging to the morning glory family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and anxiety, and acting as an antispasmodic and antimicrobial agent. Bioactive constituents of Ipomoea woronovii include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Ipomoea woronovii include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
